title: Senecio pseudolongifolius
taxonomy: Senecio pseudolongifolius belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This plant is part of the Senecio genus, a large group of flowering plants that includes ragworts and groundels. Within the Senecio genus, Senecio pseudolongifolius can be further classified as a species of shrub or perennial herb. The specific classification of Senecio pseudolongifolius includes the rank of species, genus, family, order, class, and kingdom. It is likely that Senecio pseudolongifolius is classified within the class Magnoliopsida and order Asterales.

constituents: Senecio pseudolongifolius contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which are toxic compounds found in many species of the genus Senecio. intro: Senecio pseudolongifolius, also known as False Long-leaved Ragwort, is a plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to southern Africa. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly for its potential health benefits in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Senecio pseudolongifolius include sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Senecio pseudolongifolius is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
